title: Define a real OpenClaw runtime integration surface
summary: Bridge the current workflow package into a more concrete runtime contract so docs, manifest, and examples stop implying hooks that do not yet exist.
humanSummary: Proposal to define a real runtime integration surface for eclawlution instead of relying only on workflow docs and helper scripts.
rationale: The repo currently explains a compelling adaptive system but the runtime integration surface is still thin.
A clearer contract would reduce user confusion, tighten architecture claims, and make future implementation phases more honest.
This is large enough to deserve maintainer review before implementation.
risks: Could imply hook support that OpenClaw runtime does not yet expose.
Could create docs and manifest drift if implemented partially.
Might surprise live users if automation behavior is described too concretely too early.
rollbackPlan: revert manifest and docs changes back to the current workflow-only package description
re-run local checks and examples after reverting
validationChecks: Confirm README, plugin contract, manifest, and examples all describe the same runtime surface.
Avoid auth, secrets, restart behavior, or other live side effects.
Keep the first implementation additive and local-only.
nextActions: Design a minimal hook contract for digest, self-evolution, and repo-review outputs.
Decide whether the first step is manifest/schema clarification, a combined review artifact, or actual runtime hook registration.
Review the plan with a maintainer before implementation.
